title: Persistent currents, whirlpools, and local Chern markers in twisted TMD Chern insulators
authors: Francesco Cioni, Lorenzo Cavicchi, Nazzareno Africani, Giacomo Mazza, Fabio Taddei, Amir Yacoby, Marco Polini
abstract: Recent materials advances have made it possible to fabricate twisted transition metal dichalcogenide homobilayers. These systems have been shown to host integer and fractional Chern insulating states. Because of spontaneous time reversal symmetry breaking, their ground state harbors intriguing spin-polarized currents with whirlpools on the moiré length scale that can be measured by scanning probe methods. We first provide a quantitative analysis of these persistent currents and then show that the maximum of the amplitude of the current density in the bulk of the sample is an accurate tracker of topological order. We conclude by calculating how the quantization of the Hall conductance is affected by finite-size effects.
summary:
This work investigates twisted TMD homobilayers exhibiting Chern insulating states by analyzing the ground-state current density. The authors demonstrate that the maximum local current amplitude serves as a robust, measurable marker for non-zero topological order ($C 
eq 0$). By calculating both the currents and the resulting magnetic fields, they provide theoretical evidence linking bulk topology to observable real-space electromagnetic signatures.
main_problem: To determine if a measurable local quantity exists that acts as a real-space marker for topological phases with non-zero Chern number in twisted TMD homobilayers.
main_result:
The maximum amplitude of the ground-state current density in the bulk, $\max |J(r)|$, is shown to be a reliable local proxy for detecting non-trivial Chern insulating states ($C 
eq 0$).
method: Numerical calculations are performed on a continuum model Hamiltonian describing twisted TMD homobilayers, analyzing the ground-state current density ($\mathbf{J}(\mathbf{r})$) and its associated magnetic field using the Biot-Savart law.
model_or_system: The system is modeled by twisted transition metal dichalcogenide (TMD) homobilayers hosting Chern insulating states. The physics is captured by a continuum model Hamiltonian involving intra-layer and inter-layer potentials, which breaks time-reversal symmetry.
key_observables: Local current density $\mathbf{J}(\mathbf{r})$ (exhibiting whirlpools), the maximum bulk current amplitude $\max |J(r)|$, Hall conductance ($\sigma_{xy}$), and the generated magnetic field components ($B_x, B_y, B_z$).
important_parameters: The topological invariant is the Chern number ($C$); key parameters include the twist angle ($\psi$) and ribbon width ($W$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on a continuum model description for twisted TMD homobilayers and assumes that local current density accurately tracks global topology.
figures_summary:
Figures illustrate the Topological Phase Diagram showing $C 
eq 0$ regions correlating with bulk current whirlpools. Other figures show $\sigma_{xy}$ scaling with width ($W$) and magnetic field components generated by the currents at various distances ($d$).
paper_structure: The paper progresses from establishing the local current marker ($\max |J(r)|$) in the bulk, to analyzing the contributions of intra-band vs. inter-band currents, and finally examining how global quantities like $\sigma_{xy}$ scale with finite width $W$ and how the resulting magnetic field can be measured.
why_interesting: The direct link established between a measurable local current density (a real-space observable) and a fundamental topological invariant ($C$) provides an experimental roadmap for detecting exotic quantum states in solid-state materials, which is highly relevant to condensed matter theory.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Topological phase diagram of the skyrmion Chern- band continuum model [18, 19]—panel (a)—as a function of the two parameters V and ψ in Eq. (6). White regions in- dicate topologically trivial phases while colored regions de- note phases with C ̸= 0. The triangle corresponds to twisted MoTe2. We also show the current whirlpools circulating in- side the moir´e unit cells for four different choices of param- eters. In the trivial phase—square, panel (b)—the ground- state current has a very small amplitude, while it becomes large as soon as one enters into the topological phase, panels (c) to (e). Notice the chirality reversal in passing from posi- tive (black triangle) to negative...

FIG. 2. Ground-state currents near the bottom edge of a ribbon of width W ≃72 nm of a twisted TMD homobilayer with a twist angle θ = 3.89◦. The area shaded in gray represents the edge of the ribbon. The figures depict the local current density J(r) as defined in Eq. (10) for three values of the microscopic parameter ψ corresponding to the upper band being in the: (a) trivial (ψ = 60◦) phase, (b) near-critical (ψ = 107.7◦, representing twisted MoTe2) phase, and (c) in the topological phase (ψ = 170◦). The direction of the arrows corresponds to the direction of the vector field J(r) while their color indicates the intensity. The results in this figure have been obtained by setting w = −23.8...

FIG. 3. Comparison between edge and bulk ground-state cur- rents in a twisted TMD ribbon. In both panels, the black dashed line identifies the topological phase transition. The square, triangle and circle correspond, respectively, to the three configurations in panel (a), (b), and (c) of Fig. 2, re- spectively. Panel (a) shows the average current (12) (in µA) flowing in the x-direction near the bottom edge of the ribbon, i.e. for y ∈[0, √

FIG. 4. Dependence of the Hall conductivity σxy of a twisted TMD ribbon on its width W, for various values of ψ. Data marked by orange squares refer to twisted MoTe2. For all data displayed, the Chern number is C = +1. Panel (a) shows that σxy converges to e2/h for large widths. Panel (b) shows a log-log plot of the distance between σxy and the bulk value e2/h as a function of 1/W, for large values of W (up to 35 rectangular unit cells). The dashed lines are power-law fit to the data. The color coding is the same as panel (a). All other parameters are fixed to θ = 3.89◦, w = −23.8 meV, and V = 20.8 meV.
